source_title: Where Are All The Data Centers?
other_side: NLW wants communities to move past the binary and bargain hard with a real, valuable build-out. Zitron's prior question is whether that build-out is as real and as large as everyone assumes. He spent a week hunting for big data centres that broke ground in 2023 or 2024 and were actually finished and switched on, and came up, in his words, "surprisingly empty-handed." He argues nobody has yet built a true one-gigawatt facility, that "operational" routinely describes a campus where only a handful of the planned buildings have any gear inside, and that hyperscalers refuse to disclose how much capacity is genuinely online despite over eight hundred billion dollars of capex. On his telling, the fight over a data centre's water and electricity quietly assumes a scale that the disclosures do not actually support.
truth_beneath: Both men orbit one variable: how real and how large the AI data centre build-out actually is. Both accept that data centres exist, are being built and draw real water and power, and that reporting is muddy. The empirical question that will resolve it is how much capacity has been completed and switched on; the observable that settles it is disclosed, audited operational critical-IT load - finished megawatts and gigawatts, completion timelines, honest hyperscaler figures. The residue is pure worldview: Whittemore treats the build-out as a real, valuable thing worth negotiating local benefits over; Zitron treats much of the claimed capacity as phased public-relations vapour. Whittemore is vindicated if large finished capacity is verifiably online and communities win real concessions from real campuses; Zitron if gigawatt claims keep dissolving into a few populated buildings. Both are polemicists with incentives - Whittemore bullish and anti-doom, Zitron bubble-certain and anti-hype with a newsletter - so the signal is their shared distrust of headline numbers: he doubts the capacity figures, NLW the scary impact figures. Watch whether hyperscalers ever disclose real operational capacity.
